content: Just as human language learners build long-term writing and critical thinking proficiency through repeated deliberate reflection on their own output (taught in high English curricula that name the practice reflexion), LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by borrowing the scaffolded, incremental reflection structure used in education rather than the current one-off contrastive reflection common in current AI research. This framing re-centers LLM self-reflection as a learnable skill rather than just a post-hoc output correction step.
